pages.title: Fleet Policy
pages.html:
Fleet Policy

Introduction

This Fleet Policy outlines the guidelines and procedures for the management and operation of G3-Biz Ltd’s fleet of trucks. It is aligned with the company’s vision to establish itself as the most reputable distribution company in Cameroon in terms of trust, expansion, and value creation/sharing.

Purpose

The purpose of this policy is to ensure safe, efficient, and sustainable fleet operations that minimize operational costs, maximize asset utilization, protect the company’s reputation and assets, comply with all relevant laws and regulations, and contribute to the company’s vision.

Scope

This policy applies to all drivers and vehicles owned by G3-Biz Ltd.

Key Areas

  1. Safety and Trust:
    • Prioritize Safety:Drivers must prioritize safety, adhering to traffic laws, safe driving practices, and defensive driving techniques.
    • Trustworthiness:Trustworthy behavior is essential – both on the road and with company assets.
    • Reporting:Drivers are required to promptly report any vehicle defects or malfunctions, accidents, incidents, and maintenance needs.
    • Accident Reporting:In case of an accident, drivers must follow the established accident reporting procedures.
    • Safety Meetings:Regular safety meetings will be conducted to discuss accident trends, best practices, and lessons learned.
  2. Vehicle Assignment and Usage:
    • Assignment:Trucks will be assigned based on operational requirements, driver qualifications, and vehicle capacity.
    • Authorized Drivers:Only authorized drivers with valid driving licenses and appropriate training will be permitted to operate company vehicles.
    • Business Use:Company vehicles are for business use only. Personal use is strictly prohibited.
  3. Vehicle Maintenance and Efficiency:
    • Regular Maintenance:Regular maintenance schedules will be followed for each vehicle, including routine inspections and preventive maintenance.
    • Authorized Repairs:All vehicle repairs must be authorized and performed by the approved service providers.
    • Fuel Efficiency:Fuel-efficient driving practices are encouraged to reduce costs and environmental impact.
    • Record Keeping:Accurate records of all maintenance and repair work will be maintained.
  4. Driver Training and Evaluation:
    • Comprehensive Training:All drivers will undergo comprehensive training that covers traffic laws, safe driving practices, defensive driving techniques, emergency procedures, and fuel-efficient driving.
    • Regular Evaluation:Regular performance evaluations will be conducted to assess driver skills, adherence to policies, and identify areas for improvement.
  5. Vehicle Tracking and Telematics:
    • Real-time Monitoring:Vehicles will be equipped with GPS tracking devices and telematics systems to monitor vehicle location, speed, and driving behavior.
    • Data Analysis:The collected data will be analyzed to identify potential safety issues, optimize routes, and improve fuel efficiency.
  6. Accident Investigation and Prevention:
    • Thorough Investigations:All accidents will be thoroughly investigated to determine the root causes and implement preventive measures.
    • Safety Meetings:Regular safety meetings will be held to discuss accident trends, best practices, and lessons learned.
  7. Fuel Management and Cost Control:
    • Fuel Cards:A fuel card system will be implemented to track fuel usage, monitor costs, and prevent unauthorized fuel purchases.
    • Fuel Efficiency Incentives:Drivers will be rewarded for achieving fuel efficiency targets.
  8. Vehicle Replacement and Disposal:
    • Replacement Policy:A clear vehicle replacement policy will be established based on age, mileage, and maintenance costs.
    • Disposal Procedures:Procedures for the safe and environmentally responsible disposal of old vehicles will be developed.
  9. Emergency Preparedness:
    • Emergency Kit:All vehicles will be equipped with emergency kits containing essential supplies.
    • Emergency Procedures:Emergency procedures for various scenarios, such as accidents, breakdowns, and security threats, will be developed and communicated to drivers.
  10. Sustainability and Environmental Impact:
  • Eco-Friendly Vehicles:The company will consider incorporating eco-friendly or hybrid vehicles into the fleet to reduce environmental impact.
  • Fuel Efficiency Initiatives:Efforts will be made to promote fuel-efficient driving practices and explore opportunities to reduce fuel consumption.
  1. Compliance:
  • Monitoring:Managers will monitor compliance with this policy and address any issues.
  • Disciplinary Action:Non-compliance with this policy may result in disciplinary action, up to and including termination of employment.
  • Penalties:Penalties may include written warnings, suspensions, or termination, depending on the severity of the violation.
  1. Review and Update:

Regular Review: This Fleet Policy will be reviewed annually to ensure its continued effectiveness and alignment with the company's objectives.

Vehicle Tracking and Telematics:Real-time Monitoring:Vehicles will be equipped with GPS tracking devices and telematics systems to monitor vehicle location, speed, and driving behavior.
Data Analysis:The collected data will be analyzed to identify potential safety issues, optimize routes, and improve fuel efficiency.

Accident Investigation and Prevention:Thorough Investigations:All accidents will be thoroughly investigated to determine the root causes and implement preventive measures.
Safety Meetings:Regular safety meetings will be held to discuss accident trends, best practices, and lessons learned.

Fuel Management and Cost Control:Fuel Cards:A fuel card system will be implemented to track fuel usage, monitor costs, and prevent unauthorized fuel purchases.
Fuel Efficiency Incentives:Drivers will be rewarded for achieving fuel efficiency targets.

Emergency Preparedness:Emergency Kit:All vehicles will be equipped with emergency kits containing essential supplies.
Emergency Procedures:Emergency procedures for various scenarios, such as accidents, breakdowns, and security threats, will be developed and communicated to drivers.

Sustainability and Environmental Impact:
Eco-Friendly Vehicles:The company will consider incorporating eco-friendly or hybrid vehicles into the fleet to reduce environmental impact.
Fuel Efficiency Initiatives:Efforts will be made to promote fuel-efficient driving practices and explore opportunities to reduce fuel consumption.
Compliance:
Monitoring:Managers will monitor compliance with this policy and address any issues.
Disciplinary Action:Non-compliance with this policy may result in disciplinary action, up to and including termination of employment.
Penalties:Penalties may include written warnings, suspensions, or termination, depending on the severity of the violation.
Review and Update:
Regular Review: This Fleet Policy will be reviewed annually to ensure its continued effectiveness and alignment with the company's objectives.

pages.title: Disciplinary Policy
pages.html:
Disciplinary Policy

  1. Introduction

G3 Biz Ltd is committed to maintaining a positive and professional work environment where all employees feel respected and valued. This document outlines the company's disciplinary policy, which details the procedures and sanctions associated with various types of misconduct. The goal is to ensure fair treatment for all employees and to promote a culture of mutual respect and responsibility.

  1. Definitions
  • Misconduct:Any act or omission that violates company policies, rules, regulations, or expected standards of conduct.
  • Minor Misconduct:Relatively minor transgressions, such as occasional tardiness, unauthorized absence for short durations, minor insubordination, or low-level policy violations.
  • Serious Misconduct:More severe offenses like harassment, threats, theft, fraud, violence, serious safety violations, or repeated instances of minor misconduct.
  • Gross Misconduct:The most serious offenses, including theft, cheating, lying, fraud, refusal to complete assigned duties, disclosure of confidential information, sexual harassment, or promotion of LGBTQ ideologies.
  1. Reporting Misconduct

Employees are encouraged to report any observed misconduct they believe violates company policies. There are several ways to report misconduct:

  • Directly to your immediate supervisor.
  • To the Human Resources Department.
  • Anonymously through a designated email address or message.

All reports of misconduct will be treated confidentially and investigated thoroughly. Retaliation against an employee for reporting misconduct is strictly prohibited.

  1. Investigative Procedures

Upon receiving a report of misconduct, G3 Biz Ltd will conduct a fair and impartial investigation. This may involve:

  • Interviewing the employee who reported the misconduct.
  • Interviewing the accused employee.
  • Gathering and reviewing relevant evidence.

Based on the findings of the investigation, appropriate disciplinary action will be taken.

  1. Disciplinary Procedures

G3 Biz Ltd follows a progressive disciplinary approach, escalating the severity of actions based on the nature of the misconduct.

5.1 Informal Counseling

For minor issues, direct line managers will address the concern directly with the employee through an informal verbal discussion. This provides an opportunity for clarification, correction, and performance improvement.

5.2 Query Letter:

In cases of minor misconduct, a formal Query Letter will be issued to document the incident and clearly outline the expected behavior. The employee will have 48 hours to respond in writing, acknowledging understanding of the issue and outlining steps to ensure improvement. This letter will be issued by their direct line managers approved by the HOD Human Resource and administration while informing the Depot Manager.

5.3 Warning Letter:

If a query letter is not followed by satisfactory improvement, or for initial instances of serious misconduct, a formal Warning Letter will be issued by the direct line manager. This letter will detail the misconduct, reference the specific company policy or rule that was violated, and outline clear expectations for improvement within a specified timeframe (e.g., 30 days). The employee must sign the warning letter to acknowledge receipt and understanding. A warning can also be issued as a response to an employee's response to the query letter where it applies. This is issued by the direct line manager headed by the HOD Human Resource and Administration department with approval from the Depot manager.

5.4 Disciplinary Actions:

5.4.1 Suspension: For continued misconduct after receiving a warning letter, a temporary suspension without pay may be imposed, ranging from 1 to 7 working days based on the severity of the offense and company policies.

5.4.2 Probation: In situations requiring more extensive monitoring, the employee may be placed on probation for a defined period (e.g., 60 or 90 days). During probation, performance will be closely evaluated, and further disciplinary action may be taken if improvement is not demonstrated. This decision will taken by the management team headed by the Depot manager.

5.4.3 Demotion: For severe violations or repeated instances of misconduct that do not warrant termination, demotion to a less senior role may be considered. The duration spent in the demoted role will be based on clear performance improvement criteria. This will be overseen by the management team.

5.4.4 Termination: Gross misconduct will result in immediate termination of employment. This includes but is not limited to theft, fraud, disclosure of confidential information, or serious violations of company policies. Termination will be carried out in compliance with the provisions of the Cameroon Labor Law Code and will follow due process.

  1. Appeals Process

Employees have the right to appeal any disciplinary action taken against them. Appeals must be submitted in writing to the Human Resources Department within 24 hours of receiving notification of the action.

  1. Confidentiality

All disciplinary matters will be handled with strict confidentiality to protect the privacy of all parties involved. Information will only be disclosed on a need-to-know basis and in accordance with legal requirements.

  1. Review and Modification

This policy will be reviewed periodically, at least annually, to ensure its effectiveness and may be modified as needed. Changes will be communicated to all employees.

  1. Employee Assistance Programs (EAP)

G3 Biz Ltd recognizes that personal issues can sometimes affect work performance. We offer a confidential Employee Assistance Program (EAP) to provide employees and their families with professional counseling and support services. This program can be a valuable resource for employees struggling with issues that might contribute to misconduct, such as stress, substance abuse, or relationship problems. Employees are encouraged to utilize the EAP program as a means to address personal challenges and improve their overall well-being.

  1. Training

G3 Biz Ltd is committed to fostering a positive and professional work environment. We provide training to all employees on company policies, codes of conduct, and expectations for behavior. This training helps employees understand their rights and responsibilities and promotes a culture of mutual respect and compliance.

  1. Disclaimer

This disciplinary policy document is intended to serve as a general guideline and does not constitute an employment contract. G3 Biz Ltd reserves the right to modify this policy at any time with written notice to employees.

  1. Appendix
  2. Minor Misconduct
  • Tardiness:Arriving late for work on occasional occasions (e.g., 3 times within a month).
  • Unauthorized Absence:Short, unauthorized absences from work (e.g., leaving for a personal errand without informing a supervisor).
  • Minor Insubordination:Disobeying a direct supervisor's instruction in a non-serious manner.
  • Low-Level Policy Violations:Minor breaches of company policies, such as dress code infractions or misuse of company resources (e.g., personal calls on company phones during work hours).

Consequences:

  • Verbal counseling
  • Written query letter
  • Requirement to attend relevant querry sessions
  1. Serious Misconduct
  • Repeated Tardiness: Frequent tardiness or arriving significantly late for work (e.g., 5 times within a month).
  • Unauthorized Absence (Extended): Unabsented absences from work for extended periods (e.g., one day or more) without permission.
  • Fighting: Engaging in physical altercations with colleagues or customers.
  • Refusal to Perform Duties: Refusing to complete assigned tasks without a legitimate reason.
  • Drinking Alcohol During Working Hours: Being under the influence of alcohol or consuming alcohol on company premises during work hours.
  • Harassment: Engaging in behavior that creates a hostile or offensive work environment for others, including verbal abuse, bullying, or sexual harassment.
  • Threats: Making threats of violence towards colleagues, supervisors, or customers.
  • Safety Violations: Serious violations of company safety policies that put oneself or others at risk.

Consequences:

  • Written warning letter
  • Suspension without pay (1-7 days)
  • Probationary period with close performance monitoring
  • Mandatory attendance at training programs (e.g., conflict resolution, anti-harassment)
  1. Gross Misconduct
  • Theft: Stealing company property, money, or customer goods.
  • Fraud: Deception intended for personal gain or to harm the company, such as falsifying documents or expense reports.
  • Lying/Cheating: Deliberately providing false information or engaging in dishonest practices.
  • Refusal to Sell: Refusing to complete sales transactions with customers without justification.
  • Uncompleted Route Plan: Failure to fulfill assigned tasks or complete a designated route plan without a valid reason.
  • Unauthorized Absence (Excessive): Unabsented absences for extended periods exceeding a pre-determined timeframe (e.g., more than 5 days) without permission.
  • Disclosure of Confidential Information: Sharing confidential company information with unauthorized individuals or entities.
  • Sexual Harassment: Severe or repeated instances of sexual harassment.
  • LGBTQ: spreading LGBTQ ideologies.

Consequences:

  • Immediate termination of employment. Management decision after consultation from labor office.

Please note: This appendix provides examples only. The specific consequences for misconduct will be determined on a case-by-case basis based on the severity of the offense, the employee's past disciplinary record, and other relevant factors.

pages.title: Rules and Regulations
pages.html:
Rules and Regulations

Global Strategic Business Company (G3-Biz Ltd), created on June 22nd, 2005, is a limited liability company with head office in Mile 2, Limbe – South West Region, P.O. Box 291, Limbe.

The main objects of G3-Biz Ltd are:

  1. Primary and secondary distribution of finished products.
  2. Management training/Consultancy.
  3. Offering internship training opportunities.

Article 1: Subject

  • The internal rules and regulations of G3-Biz Ltd are established in compliance with applicable laws and regulations in the Republic of Cameroon.
  • The internal rules and regulations describe the provisions including but not limited to conditions of recruitment, working conditions and remunerations, rights and obligations of workers, leave, compensations and benefits of workers, disciplinary measures, and health and safety measures.
  • Additional rules and policies of the company may be issued from time to time and communicated to the staff and posted on noticeboards in the company with all provisions of this company.

 

 

Article 2: Recruitment Policy

2.1 All applicants will be assessed through the company’s recruitment process

(HRM Planning, job analysis, job description, job advertisement and application pool) taking into consideration the basic skills required to perform the job provided the company.

2.2 Whatever the terms of recruitment and vacancy, the applicant must meet the following conditions:

  1. Having intellectual and technical requirements to fill the proposed position.
  2. Providing curriculum vitae, written application, copies of his/her academic qualifications, certificates of previous employment, copies of personal documents (NIC, NSIF, birth certificate, marriage certificate, and any other relevant document for the position). All information submitted to G3-Biz Ltd for the purpose of recruitment must be true and correct. Any falsification of information or documents submitted shall lead to the cancellation of the employment agreement for fraud; and maybe pursued for criminal action.
  3. Accepting to undergo tests and examinations, if so required. But it will be an obligation for the candidate undergoing this process for managerial positions.
  4. Providing medical certificate to prove his/her state of health which enables him/her to perform the tasks assigned for the post.
  5. Providing other relevant documents as may be required by the company for the purpose of the recruitment.
  6. An undertaken by the potential employee to respect the company culture and policies if recruited.
  7. The candidate shall undergo a selection process which may include written tests, panel interviews, aptitude tests, etc.

Article 3: Probationary Period

  • All newly recruited workers are placed under probationary period which shall not exceed three (03) months, renewable once.
  • During the probation period, the worker shall be trained to understand the company vision, culture, ways of working, rules/regulations and shall be tested on the job.
  • It is only upon a successful completion of the probation period that the worker is confirmed or else terminated.

Article 4: Working Hours

  1. Arrival/Registration/Administration: 7:00 – 7:30am (7h00 – 7h30)
  2. Management Walk around: 7:30am – 8:00am (7h30 – 8h00)
  3. Break Period: 12:30pm – 2:00pm (12h30 – 14h00)
  4. Afternoon Working Hours: 2:00 – 5:00pm (14h00 – 17h00)

Work Days: Monday – Saturday (except otherwise stated).

Beyond Work Day/Hours: The need may arise for the company to call an employee to work beyond working hours, and on public holidays. Such extra work shall earn an overtime payment to the employee.

Article 5: Overtime

Genuine, justified and pre-approved overtime shall be calculated and paid in accordance with the legislation in force; and where the nature of the job makes it complicated to do so, a fixed overtime pay of 25% of basic salary shall be paid monthly to the staff.

Article 6: Remuneration

6.1 G3-Biz Ltd shall negotiate with the worker on their remuneration (wage, salary) at the time of hiring.

6.2 Any remuneration will be paid in accordance with the company’s standard payroll procedures and in accordance with the labour/tax laws.

Article 7: Benefits

  1. Allowances:
  2. Annual Leave: in accordance with legislation.
  3. OOSA (Out of Station Allowance): Distance greater than 50km, one way.

Duration of OOSA

The OOSA excludes transport fares to be paid by the company.

OOSA duty must be approved in advance by the Line Manager.

A brief writ en report must be presented within 24 hours, especially for

overnight missions.

  1. Training:
  2. The company will identify training needs for all staff and provide the training at its cost.
  3. Training for personal development initiated by an employee may be subvented by the company to a maximum of 50% of the cost if the training programme is seen to be of benefit to the company.
  4. Social Security Scheme

In conformity with the applicable laws and regulations, the company will register all confirmed workers and pay the required contribution to the National Social Insurance Fund. The entitlement of workers under the National Social Insurance Fund is determined in accordance with applicable laws and regulations.

Article 8: Paid Annual Leave, Permission and Public Holidays

8.1 Except otherwise specifically provided by the employment agreement,

the workers are entitled to paid annual leave at the rate of one and a half workdays per month of continuous service for twelve (12) months.

8.2 Annual leave may be deferred due to company exigencies and

unforeseen circumstances; such deferment shall not exceed two (02)

years.

8.3 A worker is entitled to paid permission up to ten (10) working days

per year for the following occasions:

  1. Wedding = 3 days
  2. Paternity leave = 3 days
  3. Wedding of the worker’s child = 1 day
  4. Death of the worker’s spouse, child, and parents = 3 days

8.4 Female workers who are pregnant are entitled to maternity leave for a

total period of fourteen (14) weeks including delivery date and 1 hour

each day to breastfeed her baby for fifteen (15) months from delivery

date.

8.5 As soon as the worker knows that he/she is likely to be unable to turn

up for work due to illness, the worker must personally call the Line

Manager at least 1 hour before the start of working hours for

information and when returning to work must provide justification

documents issued by a recognized Medical Doctor.

Article 9: Permission for Unpaid Absence

9.1 Requests must be made in writing, at least seven (07) working days in

advance, except for justifiable emergencies.

9.2 Approved in writing, in advance, by the Line Manager prior to taking

absence.

NB: Permission days requested may be approved, reduced or

rejected as determined fairly by the Line Manager based on

work exigencies, and the necessity of the absence.

 

Article 10: Loss or Damage of the Company’s Property

The loss or damage to the company’s property due to misconduct or negligence of the worker shall attract disciplinary sanctions plus payment for the loss/damage by the worker.

Article 11: Access to the Company’s Premises

All workers and any other persons covered by these Internal Regulations must strictly respect the rules of access to the premises of the company. No one should be accompanied by an unauthorized person to the premises of the company without prior approval from the Depot Manager. The non-respect of such instructions will be subject to disciplinary sanctions.

Article 12: Clocking

Registration of oneself, personally, by everyone, using the system provided, to indicate the time of getting into the company premises and the time of getting out, shall be obligatory for all workers and authorized visitors/customers. For customers and visitors if not obeyed will be prohibited from entering the depot premises and for workers, we will consider workers absent for that day.

Article 13: Use of Logistical and Computer Resources

The use of G3-Biz Ltd assets for personal interest is not authorized; the use of resources, cars and trucks, internet, computer, printer (paper), and all other materials will be monitored, and any abuse may lead to appropriate disciplinary sanctions.

Article 14: Assiduity

  • Persistence
  • Effort At work, doing work!
  • Commitment
  • Attentiveness
  • Loitering/idling during working hours shall be sanctioned.

Article 15: Staff Files

Each staff must provide copies of the following documents for filling by the HOD HR/ADMIN:

  1. Job Application
  2. Employment Letter
  3. Job Responsibilities
  4. Work Contract
  5. Identity Card/Birth Certificate
  6. School Certificates
  7. Motivation/Disciplinary Letters
  8. NSIF Registration
  9. Annual leave letters, and absences

Article 16: Safety/Accident Prevention

16.1 The workers must take utmost care to ensure personal security for

each other at the workplace and shall refrain from carelessness and

any disorder which could be prejudicial to their own and others’

safety.

16.2 The workers shall immediately inform the Depot Manager or any

responsible staff of every imminent danger that they may know about.

  16.3 Safety/Security guidelines must be respected in/out of the premises.

Rules and Regulations

Global Strategic Business Company (G3-Biz Ltd), created on June 22nd, 2005, is a limited liability company with head office in Mile 2, Limbe – South West Region, P.O. Box 291, Limbe.

The main objects of G3-Biz Ltd are:

Primary and secondary distribution of finished products.
Management training/Consultancy.
Offering internship training opportunities.
Article 1: Subject

The internal rules and regulations of G3-Biz Ltd are established in compliance with applicable laws and regulations in the Republic of Cameroon.
The internal rules and regulations describe the provisions including but not limited to conditions of recruitment, working conditions and remunerations, rights and obligations of workers, leave, compensations and benefits of workers, disciplinary measures, and health and safety measures.
Additional rules and policies of the company may be issued from time to time and communicated to the staff and posted on noticeboards in the company with all provisions of this company.
 

 

Article 2: Recruitment Policy

2.1 All applicants will be assessed through the company’s recruitment process

(HRM Planning, job analysis, job description, job advertisement and application pool) taking into consideration the basic skills required to perform the job provided the company.

2.2 Whatever the terms of recruitment and vacancy, the applicant must meet the following conditions:

Having intellectual and technical requirements to fill the proposed position.
Providing curriculum vitae, written application, copies of his/her academic qualifications, certificates of previous employment, copies of personal documents (NIC, NSIF, birth certificate, marriage certificate, and any other relevant document for the position). All information submitted to G3-Biz Ltd for the purpose of recruitment must be true and correct. Any falsification of information or documents submitted shall lead to the cancellation of the employment agreement for fraud; and maybe pursued for criminal action.
Accepting to undergo tests and examinations, if so required. But it will be an obligation for the candidate undergoing this process for managerial positions.
Providing medical certificate to prove his/her state of health which enables him/her to perform the tasks assigned for the post.
Providing other relevant documents as may be required by the company for the purpose of the recruitment.
An undertaken by the potential employee to respect the company culture and policies if recruited.
The candidate shall undergo a selection process which may include written tests, panel interviews, aptitude tests, etc.
Article 3: Probationary Period

All newly recruited workers are placed under probationary period which shall not exceed three (03) months, renewable once.
During the probation period, the worker shall be trained to understand the company vision, culture, ways of working, rules/regulations and shall be tested on the job.
It is only upon a successful completion of the probation period that the worker is confirmed or else terminated.
Article 4: Working Hours

Arrival/Registration/Administration: 7:00 – 7:30am (7h00 – 7h30)
Management Walk around: 7:30am – 8:00am (7h30 – 8h00)
Break Period: 12:30pm – 2:00pm (12h30 – 14h00)
Afternoon Working Hours: 2:00 – 5:00pm (14h00 – 17h00)
Work Days: Monday – Saturday (except otherwise stated).

Beyond Work Day/Hours: The need may arise for the company to call an employee to work beyond working hours, and on public holidays. Such extra work shall earn an overtime payment to the employee.

Article 5: Overtime

Genuine, justified and pre-approved overtime shall be calculated and paid in accordance with the legislation in force; and where the nature of the job makes it complicated to do so, a fixed overtime pay of 25% of basic salary shall be paid monthly to the staff.

Article 6: Remuneration

6.1 G3-Biz Ltd shall negotiate with the worker on their remuneration (wage, salary) at the time of hiring.

6.2 Any remuneration will be paid in accordance with the company’s standard payroll procedures and in accordance with the labour/tax laws.

Article 7: Benefits

Allowances:
Annual Leave: in accordance with legislation.
OOSA (Out of Station Allowance): Distance greater than 50km, one way.
Duration of OOSA

Amount Per Category Per day (fcfa)

Junior staff

Supervisors

Management

CEO/Founder

Return same day

3,000

5,000

7,000

As per expense receipts

Overnight

15,000

18,000

22,000

The OOSA excludes transport fares to be paid by the company.

OOSA duty must be approved in advance by the Line Manager.

A brief writ en report must be presented within 24 hours, especially for

overnight missions.

Training:
The company will identify training needs for all staff and provide the training at its cost.
Training for personal development initiated by an employee may be subvented by the company to a maximum of 50% of the cost if the training programme is seen to be of benefit to the company.
Social Security Scheme
In conformity with the applicable laws and regulations, the company will register all confirmed workers and pay the required contribution to the National Social Insurance Fund. The entitlement of workers under the National Social Insurance Fund is determined in accordance with applicable laws and regulations.

Article 8: Paid Annual Leave, Permission and Public Holidays

8.1 Except otherwise specifically provided by the employment agreement,

the workers are entitled to paid annual leave at the rate of one and a half workdays per month of continuous service for twelve (12) months.

8.2 Annual leave may be deferred due to company exigencies and

unforeseen circumstances; such deferment shall not exceed two (02)

years.

8.3 A worker is entitled to paid permission up to ten (10) working days

per year for the following occasions:

Wedding = 3 days
Paternity leave = 3 days
Wedding of the worker’s child = 1 day
Death of the worker’s spouse, child, and parents = 3 days
8.4 Female workers who are pregnant are entitled to maternity leave for a

total period of fourteen (14) weeks including delivery date and 1 hour

each day to breastfeed her baby for fifteen (15) months from delivery

date.

8.5 As soon as the worker knows that he/she is likely to be unable to turn

up for work due to illness, the worker must personally call the Line

Manager at least 1 hour before the start of working hours for

information and when returning to work must provide justification

documents issued by a recognized Medical Doctor.

Article 9: Permission for Unpaid Absence

9.1 Requests must be made in writing, at least seven (07) working days in

advance, except for justifiable emergencies.

9.2 Approved in writing, in advance, by the Line Manager prior to taking

absence.

NB: Permission days requested may be approved, reduced or

rejected as determined fairly by the Line Manager based on

work exigencies, and the necessity of the absence.

 

Article 10: Loss or Damage of the Company’s Property

The loss or damage to the company’s property due to misconduct or negligence of the worker shall attract disciplinary sanctions plus payment for the loss/damage by the worker.

Article 11: Access to the Company’s Premises

All workers and any other persons covered by these Internal Regulations must strictly respect the rules of access to the premises of the company. No one should be accompanied by an unauthorized person to the premises of the company without prior approval from the Depot Manager. The non-respect of such instructions will be subject to disciplinary sanctions.

Registration of oneself, personally, by everyone, using the system provided, to indicate the time of getting into the company premises and the time of getting out, shall be obligatory for all workers and authorized visitors/customers. For customers and visitors if not obeyed will be prohibited from entering the depot premises and for workers, we will consider workers absent for that day.

The use of G3-Biz Ltd assets for personal interest is not authorized; the use of resources, cars and trucks, internet, computer, printer (paper), and all other materials will be monitored, and any abuse may lead to appropriate disciplinary sanctions.

pages.title: Archiving Guidelines and Procedures Policy
pages.html:
Archiving Guidelines and Procedures Policy

Purpose

The archiving guidelines and procedures policy of G3 Biz Ltd aims to establish a comprehensive system for the efficient organization, storage, retention, and retrieval of essential company documents and records. This policy is designed to ensure compliance with legal and regulatory requirements, safeguard sensitive information, and optimize operational efficiency, aligning with G3 Biz Ltd’s vision to be one of the most reputable distribution companies in Cameroon in terms of trust, expansion, value creation, and growth.

Scope

This policy encompasses the archiving of all critical company documents, including but not limited to:

  • Human Resources:Employee files, contracts, job descriptions, salary records, performance evaluations, leave records, disciplinary actions.
  • Accounts:Financial statements, invoices, receipts, tax returns, payroll records, expense reports.
  • Operations:Procurement documents, inventory records, maintenance logs, quality control reports.
  • Legal and Compliance:Contracts, legal correspondence, permits, licenses, insurance policies.
  • Company Policies:Internal policies, procedures, and guidelines, rules and regulations.

Document Management and Labeling

All documents generated or received by G3 Biz Ltd must be:

  • Properly titled:Descriptive and accurately reflecting the document's content.
  • Clearly labeled:With relevant information such as document type, creation date, author, and department.
  • Dated and time-stamped:To facilitate accurate record-keeping.
  • Organized:In a logical and accessible filing system.

Document Format and Storage

  • Electronic Documents:Preferred format for new documents to enhance accessibility, search capabilities, and storage efficiency.
  • Acceptable Electronic Formats:PDF, Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint), and other industry-standard formats.
  • Paper Documents:May be retained for specific legal or operational requirements. Should be scanned and converted to electronic format when possible.
  • Electronic Document Management System (EDMS):Implement a robust EDMS to securely store, manage, and retrieve electronic documents.
  • Data Backup:Regular backups of electronic documents should be performed and stored in a secure off-site location eg a drive, a flash, and even cloud storage, external hardrive.

Responsibilities

The Head of Department HR/Administration is responsible for overseeing the archiving process and ensuring adherence to this policy. Department heads and employees are accountable for creating and maintaining accurate records as per their departments.

 

Retention Schedule

All archived documents will be retained for a minimum of 10 years unless otherwise required by law. A detailed retention schedule will be developed and maintained to outline specific retention periods for different document types.

Storage and Security

  • Physical Storage:Archived paper documents will be securely stored in designated, locked storage areas accessible only to authorized personnel.
  • Access Control:Access to both physical and electronic documents is restricted to the Accounts, Head of Department HR/Administration, Depot Manager, and Managing Director.
  • Confidentiality:All archived documents, both physical and digital, contain confidential information and must be handled with utmost care.
  • Data Privacy and Protection:Implement measures to protect personal and sensitive information, including data encryption, access controls, and employee training on data protection practices.
  • Disaster Recovery:A disaster recovery plan will be in place to protect archived documents from loss or damage.

Document Retrieval

To access archived documents, employees must submit a written request to the Head of Department HR/Administration, specifying the required information. Requests will be processed promptly, and copies of documents may be provided, subject to authorization.

Review and Updates

This archiving policy will be reviewed annually to ensure its effectiveness and compliance with changing legal and regulatory requirements and to align with G3 Biz Ltd’s vision of becoming one of the most reputable distribution companies in Cameroon.

pages.title: Procurement Policy
pages.html:
Procurement Policy

  1. 1. Introduction

This procurement policy outlines the principles, procedures, and guidelines for the acquisition of goods, services, and works by G3 Biz Limited. It aims to ensure efficient, economical, and transparent procurement practices that support the company's strategic objectives.

 

  1. 2. Scope

This policy applies to all procurement activities undertaken by G3 Biz Limited, including but not limited to the purchase of heavy-duty trucks, spare parts, mechanical services, office utilities, and administrative utilities.

 

  1. 3. Procurement Objectives
  • - To procure goods and services of the highest quality at the most competitive prices.
  • - To ensure timely delivery of goods and services to meet operational requirements.
  • - To build and maintain strong relationships with reliable and ethical suppliers.
  • - To comply with all applicable laws, regulations, and industry standards.
  • - To minimize procurement risks and maximize value for the company.

 

  1. 4. Procurement Principles
  • - Integrity and Transparency: All procurement activities shall be conducted with honesty, integrity, and transparency.
  • - Fairness and Impartiality: All suppliers will be treated fairly and impartially.
  • - Competition: Competitive bidding will be encouraged whenever feasible to achieve the best value for money.
  • - Value for Money: Procurement decisions will be based on a comprehensive evaluation of cost, quality, and performance.
  • - Sustainability: G3 Biz will consider environmental and social impacts in its procurement decisions.

 

  1. 5. Supplier Selection and Management
  • - Supplier Registration: All potential suppliers must register with G3 Biz to be considered for business.
  • - Supplier Evaluation: Suppliers will be evaluated based on criteria such as financial stability, quality, delivery performance, and ethical practices.
  • - Supplier Performance: Supplier performance will be monitored through regular evaluations and feedback.
  • - Supplier Relationship Management: G3 Biz will foster long-term relationships with key suppliers based on mutual trust and cooperation.

 

  1. 6. Procurement Process
  • - Requisition: Departments will submit purchase requisitions for required goods and services to their line mangers.
  • - Purchase Order: A purchase order will be issued to the approved supplier upon authorization following guidelines put in place by the Accounts department.
  • - Delivery and Acceptance: Goods and services will be inspected upon delivery to ensure compliance with specifications by line managers and confirmed in the procurement department (Human resource).
  • - Payment: Payments will be made in accordance with the terms and conditions of the purchase order.
  • - Competitive Bidding: For purchases exceeding one hundred thousand (100,000) FCFA, G3 Biz will solicit price quotations from at least three (3) qualified suppliers. The procurement department will evaluate these quotations based on price, quality, delivery terms, and supplier performance.
  • - Payment Terms: All payments made by G3 Biz will be processed through cheque when they exceed 100,000frs CFA. Suppliers must provide their complete bank details, including account number, bank name, and branch, for the processing of payments.
  • - Supplier Documentation: Suppliers must submit a valid tax payer identification number (TPIN) as proof of tax compliance. This document will be verified before the establishment of a business relationship.

 

  1. 7. Inventory Management
  • - G3 Biz will maintain optimal inventory levels to balance supply and demand.
  • - Inventory will be regularly reviewed and adjusted to minimize carrying costs and stockouts.
  • - Inventory control systems will be implemented to track and monitor inventory levels.

 

  1. 8. Contract Management
  • - Contracts will be drafted clearly and comprehensively, outlining the terms and conditions of the agreement.
  • - Contract performance will be monitored to ensure compliance.
  • - Contract amendments will be made in writing and approved by authorized personnel.

 

  1. 9. Cost Management
  • - Cost analysis will be conducted to identify cost-saving opportunities.
  • - Price comparisons will be made to ensure competitive pricing.
  • - Volume discounts and other cost-saving measures will be explored.

 

  1. 10. Risk Management
  • - Potential procurement risks will be identified and assessed.
  • - Risk mitigation strategies will be developed and implemented.
  • - Insurance coverage will be considered for significant procurement risks.

 

  1. 11. Compliance
  • - G3 Biz will comply with all applicable laws, regulations, and industry standards.
  • - Regular compliance audits will be conducted.

 

  1. 12. Ethical Sourcing
  • - G3 Biz will promote ethical and sustainable procurement practices.
  • - Suppliers will be expected to adhere to G3 Biz's code of conduct.

 

  1. 13. Procurement Organization
  • - Department in charge of procurement is the department for human resource and administration overseen by the depot manager.
  •  

This procurement policy serves as a framework for G3 Biz's procurement activities. It may be amended from time to time to reflect changes in business needs and legal requirements.

posts.title: Inside G3-BIZ: The Power of Our Tuesday Presentation Meetings
posts.html:
At G3-BIZ, every Tuesday holds a significant place in our weekly routine. It’s more than just a day for work—it’s the day we come together as a team to reflect, learn, and push the boundaries of what we can achieve. Our Tuesday presentation meetings serve as the cornerstone of our sales strategy, where we review progress, share insights, and ensure every member of the team is aligned with our goals.

Here’s what a typical Tuesday meeting looks like at G3-BIZ:

Setting the Tone with Prayers and Introductions: We believe in starting our meetings on the right note. Each session begins with a moment of reflection and prayers, followed by introductions. This not only sets a positive and focused atmosphere but also reinforces the unity we aim to maintain within the team. Erectile dysfunction at age 30 concerns many men, often attributed to stress or physiological factors. Treatments include lifestyle adjustments and medication. Interactions between sildenafil and alcohol may affect efficacy. It’s a reminder that we’re all in this together, working toward common goals.

Focus on Safety and Security: One of our core values at G3-BIZ is ensuring the safety and security of our team members, especially those working on the front lines of sales. We kick off the meeting by addressing any safety and security updates to ensure that every team member remains informed and protected while out in the field. Sales activities often present unpredictable situations, so staying updated on best practices helps us maintain efficiency without compromising safety.

  1. Sales Review: A Comprehensive Look at Performance: The heart of our Tuesday meeting revolves around a detailed sales review. This begins with a general presentation where we examine the overall sales performance, identifying key trends and results. From there, our Business Promoters take the lead, presenting their respective sales teams’ achievements. This is where each sales team gets the opportunity to share:
  • Sales Targets: What their objectives were for the week.
  • Achievements: How close they came to meeting or exceeding their targets.
  • Challenges: The hurdles they encountered in the market, whether it’s competition, supply chain issues, or customer engagement challenges.
  • Opportunities: Areas where they see potential for growth or improvement.
  • Market Trends: Key shifts in consumer behavior, demand, and industry dynamics.
  • Teamwork: How collaboration within and between teams has driven results.

This segment is crucial as it allows us to not only review past performance but also develop strategies for future improvements.

Feedback and Insights from Leadership: After each sales team presents, the meeting opens up for reactions from key leadership figures, including the Sales Supervisor, Depot Manager, and other staff members. These insights offer a wealth of feedback and guidance, reinforcing what’s working and highlighting areas for improvement.

The Sales Supervisor plays a pivotal role here, emphasizing the importance of Sales Driver Checks—a vital strategy used by the sales teams to pull in more crates. These checks help us remain proactive in identifying potential sales opportunities, ensuring that our teams stay ahead in the market.

The Depot Manager also provides valuable input, addressing the staff directly to ensure that operational issues are minimized and team morale is high.

Safety, Security, and Sales: In addition to performance discussions, the Sales Supervisor reinforces the importance of maintaining safety and security in all sales activities. This ensures that the sales team is equipped to handle any challenges in the field while keeping their well-being a priority.

Addressing Other Matters and Staff Concerns: A vital part of every meeting is giving the floor to our team members to raise any concerns or suggestions. Whether it’s operational issues, market challenges, or personal requests, this open forum allows for transparent communication. By addressing staff concerns, we ensure that our team feels heard, valued, and empowered to share their perspectives.

The Depot War Song: A G3-BIZ Tradition: No Tuesday meeting at G3-BIZ is complete without the singing of our Depot War Song. It’s more than just a tradition—it’s a source of motivation and pride for the entire team. As the song resonates through the room, it’s a reminder of our shared mission and the collective energy that drives us forward.

Here are the lyrics to our Depot War Song:

Guinness, a drink for every Cameroonian
Arise, employees of G3-Biz
Promote and spread Guinness nationwide
Guinness is great for all

Zimbabala, Big Guinness
Zimbabala, Small Guinness
Zimbabala!

This song serves as a unifying moment, inspiring us to approach our goals with passion and reminding us of the vital role we play in promoting Guinness products across the nation.

The Power of Teamwork at G3-BIZ

Our Tuesday presentation meetings are more than just routine—they’re a testament to the power of teamwork, communication, and continuous improvement. These meetings enable us to align our goals, share valuable insights, and ensure that each team member is equipped with the tools and strategies needed to succeed in the fast-paced world of sales.

At G3-BIZ, we don’t just meet—we grow, we learn, and we push each other to achieve more, together.

posts.title: Unlock Your Future with G3-BIZ: Internship Opportunities for Students in Business Administration, HR, Accounting, and More
posts.html:
At G3-BIZ, we believe that the key to building a strong future workforce lies in nurturing young talent. That’s why we’re proud to offer exciting internship opportunities for students who are ready to take their first steps into the professional world. Whether you're studying Business Administration, Human Resources Management, Accounting, Management, Sales, or a related field, our internship program is designed to give you hands-on experience and a platform for growth.

Why Intern at G3-BIZ?

Internships are about more than just gaining credits or padding your resume—they’re about learning, growing, and positioning yourself for long-term success. At G3-BIZ, we don't just give you tasks to keep you busy; we provide a structured learning environment where your ideas are valued, and your contributions make a difference.

Here’s why G3-BIZ is the ideal place to kickstart your career:

- Real-World Experience: As an intern, you’ll be immersed in the daily operations of a dynamic global company. From working on live projects to shadowing seasoned professionals, you’ll gain insights that can’t be found in a classroom.

- Mentorship and Guidance: Our team is dedicated to helping you succeed. Throughout your internship, you’ll work closely with mentors who are experts in their respective fields. They’ll guide you, provide feedback, and help you grow both personally and professionally.

- Diverse Opportunities: Whether you’re interested in business administration, HR, accounting, or sales, we have a wide range of opportunities tailored to your area of study. You’ll get a comprehensive understanding of your chosen field while also being exposed to other key business functions.

- Networking and Collaboration: At G3-BIZ, we believe in collaboration. You’ll have the chance to work alongside professionals and peers across various departments, giving you a chance to build a strong network and learn from different perspectives.

Internships in Key Areas:

At the core of our activities is a blend of full supply chain activities because our customers are at the center of our activities. Get to experience the flows within our activities that range from information, physical, and financial flows.

At G3-Biz, you will get to hands-on desk training from procurement from our upper streams to inbound logistics, operations, outbound logistics, sales and distribution, and customer service.
Your training is sure with us and we help you advise on your professional career and also why not work with us.

1. Business Administration

Learn the ins and outs of managing a global distribution company. As a business administration intern, you’ll assist with operations, project management, and strategic planning. You’ll see firsthand how decisions are made and how various business functions come together to drive success.

2. Human Resources Management

Interested in shaping the future of a company’s workforce? As an HR intern, you’ll assist with recruitment, onboarding, employee engagement, and more. You’ll get to see how HR plays a critical role in maintaining a positive work culture and how it aligns with the overall business strategy.

3. Accounting

For those with a passion for numbers, our accounting internship offers hands-on experience in financial reporting, budgeting, and auditing. You’ll work closely with our finance team to ensure the smooth management of our financial operations and gain critical insights into financial decision-making.

4. Management

As a management intern, you’ll be involved in the decision-making processes that impact every level of the business. You’ll learn about leadership, organizational behavior, and how to balance short-term tasks with long-term strategic goals.

5. Sales and Marketing

If you’re passionate about driving growth, a sales and marketing internship is for you. You’ll assist in developing strategies, conducting market research, and identifying new opportunities to expand our reach. This internship will sharpen your skills in customer relationship management and sales techniques.

What You’ll Gain

- Hands-On Learning: Real projects, real challenges, and real results.
- Industry Knowledge: Learn from a company at the forefront of global distribution.
- Professional Development: Gain skills that will set you apart in the job market.
- Networking Opportunities: Build lasting connections with professionals in your field.
- Pathway to Full-Time Employment: Many of our interns go on to become full-time employees at G3-BIZ, having proved themselves during their internships.

Unlock Your Future with G3-BIZ

At G3-BIZ, we’re committed to helping students develop the skills they need to succeed in today’s competitive business world. Our internships provide the perfect opportunity to gain practical experience, build a professional network, and set the foundation for a successful career. Join us, and let’s shape the future together.

posts.title: G3-BIZ Corporate Social Responsibility: Giving Back to the Community
posts.html:
At G3-BIZ, we believe that success in business goes hand-in-hand with making a positive impact on the communities in which we operate. As a responsible corporate entity, we are committed to giving back in various ways—from education and healthcare to environmental sustainability and professional development. Our Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) initiatives are designed to uplift and support our communities while creating a lasting, positive impact.

1. Healthcare Initiatives

One of the key areas of focus for G3-BIZ’s CSR program is healthcare. We recognize the importance of good health as the foundation of a thriving society. That’s why we’ve made it a priority to support local healthcare initiatives through donations, health awareness campaigns etc

.Our commitment is to promote healthier living and raise awareness about preventive care.

2. Supporting Education and Professional Development

Education is the foundation of a strong future, and G3-BIZ is committed to advancing educational opportunities in our communities. We provide scholarships, donate learning materials, and support local schools to ensure access to quality education. Our goal is to inspire and equip the next generation of leaders with the tools they need to succeed academically and professionally.

In addition to traditional education support, G3-BIZ offers internship opportunities to students in fields such as Business Administration, Human Resources Management, Accounting, Management, Sales, and more. These internships provide hands-on experience, allowing students to apply what they’ve learned in the classroom to real-world business situations. By mentoring and training young professionals, we help bridge the gap between education and employment, preparing them to excel in the competitive job market.

3. Community Engagement Days: A Cleaner Limbe

As part of our commitment to the environment and the local community, G3-BIZ actively participates in the “Keep Clean” campaign every first Wednesday of the month in the city of Limbe. This monthly community engagement day is focused on keeping the city clean, healthy, and safe for its residents.

Our employees join forces with other businesses, city officials, and residents to clean up streets, public spaces, and neighborhoods. This initiative helps to reduce litter, clear drains, and improve the overall cleanliness of Limbe. We believe that a cleaner environment fosters better living conditions, and our participation in this campaign is a reflection of our dedication to the community's well-being.

4. Sustainability and Environmental Responsibility

G3-BIZ is also dedicated to minimizing our environmental footprint through responsible business practices. We incorporate sustainability into our day-to-day operations by promoting efficient waste management, encouraging recycling, and ensuring that our business processes align with environmentally friendly standards. Whether through our logistics operations or our community initiatives, we are always looking for ways to make our business more sustainable.

5. Empowering Through Logistics

As a global distribution company, logistics is a cornerstone of our operations. We take pride in offering efficient services that benefit both our customers and the community at large.

This streamlined approach ensures that our operations not only support the growth of local businesses but also contribute to the broader economy by ensuring the timely and efficient distribution of products. Through our logistics services, we create jobs, support supply chain resilience, and foster local business development.

6. Empowering Local Talent Through Employment and Internships

At G3-BIZ, we don't just believe in hiring talent—we believe in nurturing it. We are proud to offer local employment opportunities and internships that provide real-world experience to students and young professionals. Our goal is to help young people gain valuable skills that will benefit them throughout their careers while also contributing to the local economy.

Our internship program spans multiple departments, including business administration, HR, accounting, management, and sales, offering hands-on learning that helps bridge the gap between education and employment. Many of our interns have gone on to become full-time employees, having proven themselves during their internship period.

 

Through our various CSR activities, G3-BIZ is not just doing business—we’re building stronger, healthier, and more sustainable communities. We’re proud to contribute to the well-being of the people and the environment, and we invite others to join us in these efforts. Together, we can make a difference.

posts.title: G3-Biz Ltd Relaunches with a Bold New Vision
posts.html: On June 12, 2024, G3-Biz Ltd marked a significant milestone with a transformative relaunch. This event introduced a refreshed vision and mission aimed at reshaping the company's future, making it a prominent player in the distribution industry of Cameroon. The new vision is clear: Establishing G3-Biz Ltd as the most reputable distribution company in Cameroon in terms of trust, expansion, value creation, and value sharing. This vision emphasizes not just achieving impressive sales figures but creating lasting value for all stakeholders—from customers to shareholders. ### Vision At the core of G3-Biz’s new direction is the focus on becoming a trusted and expansive distributor, dedicated to: - Giving customers good value for their investment. - Creating occasions that enhance social interaction and pleasure - Offering trustful partnerships to suppliers - Providing a better life for shareholders and employees Managing Director Moses Fongang highlights the company’s goal of encouraging employees to work harder and make more money, with an emphasis on 100% compliance, creativity and innovation, and a heightened sense of responsibility across all operations. ### Strategic Pillars G3-Biz Ltd is driving this new vision through four strategic pillars: 1. Human Resource Optimization – Ensuring the workforce is efficient, motivated, and aligned with company goals. 2. Exponential Volume Growth– Scaling operations and increasing distribution capacity. 3. Market Share Growth– Expanding G3-Biz’s presence and influence in Cameroon’s market. 4. Wise Investment in Assets – Making informed, strategic investments that secure long-term success. ### Shifting to New Ways of Working Before the relaunch, G3-Biz’s operations had faced challenges such as non-compliance and a *lack of integrity due to a more laid-back working culture. The new vision, however, aims to transform this by instilling a culture of value creation in every sale. It’s no longer just about selling but about ensuring customers see real value in their investment. Fongang underscores the importance of maintaining strict compliance, fostering innovation, and taking responsibility at every level, ensuring that both employees and customers benefit from these changes. A Promising Future With a clear vision and strategic framework in place, G3-Biz Ltd is set to redefine the distribution landscape in Cameroon. By prioritizing trust, value creation, and integrity, the company is committed to fostering long-term success and shared growth for all its stakeholders. As G3-Biz embarks on this exciting new journey, it is determined to build a future where purpose, responsibility, and success go hand in hand.

- Giving customers good value for their investment.
- Creating occasions that enhance social interaction and pleasure
- Offering trustful partnerships to suppliers
- Providing a better life for shareholders and employees
Managing Director Moses Fongang highlights the company’s goal of encouraging employees to work harder and make more money, with an emphasis on 100% compliance, creativity and innovation, and a heightened sense of responsibility across all operations.
### Strategic Pillars
G3-Biz Ltd is driving this new vision through four strategic pillars:
1. Human Resource Optimization – Ensuring the workforce is efficient, motivated, and aligned with company goals.
2. Exponential Volume Growth– Scaling operations and increasing distribution capacity.
3. Market Share Growth– Expanding G3-Biz’s presence and influence in Cameroon’s market.
4. Wise Investment in Assets – Making informed, strategic investments that secure long-term success.
### Shifting to New Ways of Working
Before the relaunch, G3-Biz’s operations had faced challenges such as non-compliance and a *lack of integrity due to a more laid-back working culture. The new vision, however, aims to transform this by instilling a culture of value creation in every sale. It’s no longer just about selling but about ensuring customers see real value in their investment.
Fongang underscores the importance of maintaining strict compliance, fostering innovation, and taking responsibility at every level, ensuring that both employees and customers benefit from these changes.
A Promising Future
With a clear vision and strategic framework in place, G3-Biz Ltd is set to redefine the distribution landscape in Cameroon. By prioritizing trust, value creation, and integrity, the company is committed to fostering long-term success and shared growth for all its stakeholders.
As G3-Biz embarks on this exciting new journey, it is determined to build a future where purpose, responsibility, and success go hand in hand.

posts.title: 🌟 Women Crush Wednesday: Celebrating Mme Tayimfor Therese! 🌟
posts.html:
🌟 Women Crush Wednesday: Celebrating Mme Tayimfor Therese! 🌟

Today, at G3-biz Ltd, we want to take a moment to shine a spotlight on an incredible woman who inspires us all—our very own depot manager, Mme Tayimfor Therese! With over 10 years of experience in the business, she has beautifully grown from a sales lady to the powerhouse leader she is today.

Through her journey, she has faced challenges with remarkable resilience and persistence. Whether it’s during the tough times or the periods of growth, she has shown nothing but commitment and discipline. Some days she’s sweet, some days she’s a bit sour, and other days she’s wonderfully bittersweet—yet each facet of her personality contributes to her being an all-round leader.

We affectionately call her "mami Guiness" because she embodies strength and grace in everything she does. Today, let’s give her the flowers she truly deserves for being a lioness in our midst!

Thank you, Mme Tayimfor Therese, for your unwavering dedication and for leading us with such passion. We are grateful to have you as our guiding light! 💖🌹

Thank you, Mme Tayimfor Therese, for your unwavering dedication and for leading us with such passion. We are grateful to have you as our guiding light! 💖🌹

Tadagra: What to Know, Warnings, Results
posts.html:
Pharmaceutical advancements have paved the way for improved management of erectile dysfunction (ED). Tadagra, an ED medication, has gained traction for its efficacy and safety profile. This article delves into how Tadagra functions, its off-label applications, and more.

How Does Tadagra Work

The active ingredient in Tadagra is tadalafil. This compound belongs to the phosphodiesterase type 5 (PDE5) inhibitors class. Tadagra 20mg enhances blood flow to the penis, facilitating a sustained erection. It achieves this by relaxing the blood vessels in the penile region, crucial for erectile function.

PDE5 inhibitors, like Tadagra, obstruct the degradation of cyclic guanosine monophosphate (cGMP). This biochemical allows smooth muscle relaxation in the corpus cavernosum. Consequently, this promotes increased blood inflow upon sexual arousal. Tadagra does not initiate an erection; it enhances the natural response to sexual stimulation.

Off-Label Uses

Though primarily prescribed for ED, Tadagra's pharmacodynamics have shown potential in addressing other health issues. One such application involves the management of benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH). Tadalafil aids in alleviating urinary symptoms associated with BPH by relaxing the smooth muscles in the prostate and bladder.

Another emerging use involves the treatment of pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH). Tadalafil 20mg assists in reducing pulmonary vascular resistance, improving exercise capacity in individuals with PAH. Its efficacy in treating these conditions underscores its versatility beyond erectile dysfunction.

Tadagra Contraindications

Not all patients can safely use Tadagra. Contraindications include individuals with hypersensitivity to tadalafil. Concurrent use with nitrates or alpha-blockers poses significant risks, such as severe hypotension.

Patients with cardiovascular conditions require careful evaluation before initiating Tadagra. Those with severe heart disease, recent stroke, or myocardial infarction must abstain from its use. It is vital to consult healthcare professionals before incorporating Tadagra into any treatment plan.

Missed Dose

Adherence to the prescribed dosage is crucial. If a dose is missed, take Tadagra 20mg as soon as possible. If it is close to the next dose, skip the missed dose. Avoid double dosing to prevent adverse outcomes.

Consistent administration ensures optimal therapeutic effects. Maintain a regular dosing schedule and consult your healthcare provider if uncertainties arise.

Tadagra Adverse Effects

While Tadagra 20mg is effective, it is not without side effects. Common adverse effects include headaches, flushing, and dyspepsia. Some individuals may experience nasal congestion or back pain.

Rare, but severe, effects like sudden vision loss or priapism demand immediate medical attention. Consult a healthcare professional for any adverse reactions. An understanding of potential risks enhances the safe use of Tadagra.

Tadagra For Sale Online

Purchasing Tadagra online offers convenience, but caution is essential. Ensure purchases are from reputable pharmacies. Authenticity and safety must be priorities when acquiring medications online.

Check for a valid prescription requirement, as legitimate vendors will request one. Verify the pharmacy’s credentials through regulatory bodies, such as the FDA. This vigilance protects against counterfeit medications, ensuring therapeutic efficacy.

Dosage Application
Tadagra 20mg Erectile Dysfunction
Tadalafil 20mg Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension

For safe use of Tadagra, consider the following:

  • Consult a healthcare provider.
  • Ensure reputable sources for online purchases.
  • Be aware of contraindications and interactions.

Awareness of Tadagra's mechanism of action, potential uses, and precautions enhances its safe and effective application in clinical practice. Proper adherence and consultation can ensure optimal therapeutic outcomes.

posts.title: Launching of the new Guinness Smooth GES 65cl x12
posts.html:
G3-Biz Ltd Partners with Guinness Cameroon to Launch the New Guinness Smooth GES 65cl x12 in Limbe
As one of the trusted partners of Guinness Cameroon, G3-Biz Ltd is proud to join in the launch of the new Guinness Smooth GES 65cl x12, right here in the vibrant city of Limbe.
This launch marks the 20th anniversary of the Guinness Smooth™ brand, a symbol of quality, richness, and consumer satisfaction. In celebration of this milestone, Guinness Cameroon introduces the GES 65cl—a larger size offering the same distinctive smoothness that consumers have loved over the years.
At G3-Biz Ltd, we remain committed to delivering value to our customers, and this new format is a reward for their loyalty to the Guinness Smooth brand. It’s bigger, bolder, and just as smooth.
We’re excited to continue building this journey with Guinness Cameroon, bringing trusted products and rewarding experiences to our community.

posts.html:
A) JOB VACANCY ADVERTISEMENT
  • - Position: Human Resource Manager
  • - Company: G3 Biz Ltd
  • - Location: Mile 2 Limbe, South West Region
B) About G3 Biz Ltd
G3 Biz Ltd is a reputable distribution company that represents Guinness Cameroun in Limbe. The company is primarily engaged in the distribution and marketing of Guinness Cameroon products, offering internship programs to undergraduates, and providing business consultancy services. Its vision is to establish G3 Biz Ltd as the most reputable company in Cameroon in terms of trust, expansion, value creation and value sharing. It has a dedicated workforce of 40 permanent staff.
C) Strategic Role Objective of Human Resource Manager
  • - The Human Resource Manager will play a pivotal role in achieving the company’s vision by ensuring that the company attracts, develops, and retains a highly motivated and competent workforce.
D) Some Specific Role Responsibilities of the HR Manager:
  • - Develop and implement HR strategies aligned with the company’s overall strategic goals and vision.
  • - Promote a positive organizational culture that fosters productivity, teamwork, and continuous improvement.
  • - Strengthen employee relations and ensure compliance with labor laws and company policies.
  • - Drive performance management and capacity-building initiatives to enhance staff efficiency.
  • - Support management in strategic workforce planning and talent development
E) Qualifications and Requirements
  • - Bachelor’s in HR Management, or in Business Management, or in the equivavent.
  • - Minimum of 3–5 years of experience in a similar HR leadership role.
  • - Strong knowledge of labor laws and human resource best practices.
  • - Excellent interpersonal, communication, and leadership skills.
  • - Proficiency in Microsoft Office and HR management software.
  • - Demonstrated ability to work independently and in a team-oriented environment.
F) How to Apply
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and cover letter addressed to: The Managing Director G3 Biz Ltd, Limbe. Website address: www.g3-bizltd.com Website email: info@g3-bizltd.com Or drop your hard copy directly to the depot manager. 
"Deadline to submit application: 21st October 2025"

posts.html:
JOB VACANCY 1.GENERAL INFORMATION Solicitation Number:G3-Biz/HR/01/03/2026 Issuance Date:31/03/2026 Closing Date/Time:14/04/2026 Point of Contact:info@g3-biz ltd.com Position Title/Number Needed:Marketing Officer (01) Duty Type:Full Time Reporting Line:Management.   2.STATEMENT OF DUTY Global Strategic Business Limited(G3-Biz Ltd) is a dynamic distribution company committed to disciplined market expansion,strong brand positioning and responsible commercial execution. We are seeking a result -driven and innovative marketing officer who will strengthen product visibility,drive market engagement,and support sales growth while upholding the company"s values of integrity,accountability,professionalism and teamwork.

3. ROLE PURPOSE

To drive brand visibility, customer engagement, and market expansion through practical marketing initiatives, while supporting sales performance and strengthening the company’s market presence across assigned territories.

4. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

  1. Market Activation & Brand Visibility
  • Plan and execute field marketing activities and promotions
  • Ensure strong product visibility and merchandising standards across outlets
  • Identify and leverage market opportunities, events, and campaigns
  1. Sales Support, Market Development& Market Share Growth
  • Work closely with sales teams to drive demand and product movement
  • Support achievement of sales and distribution targets
  • Contribute to new outlet activation and market expansion efforts
  • Identify gaps and implement actions to improve competitive positioning in the markets.
  1. Market Intelligence & Reporting
  • Monitor competitor activities, pricing trends, and customer behavior
  • Provide timely and accurate marketing and field reports
  • Track performance of campaigns and recommend improvements
  1. Consumer Driven Activities& Brand Representation
  • Build and maintain strong customer and partner relationships
  • Ensure consistent and professional brand representation in the market
  • Support customer loyalty and retention initiatives

5. PERSONAL ETHICS & BEHAVIOURAL EXPECTATIONS

We are recruiting not just skill, but character. The ideal candidate must:
  • Demonstrate integrity and accountability in all activities
  • Show initiative and ability to work with minimal supervision
  • Maintain discipline and professionalism in the field
  • Be adaptable, proactive, and solution-oriented
  • Uphold and protect the company’s brand image at all times

6. MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

Education: Bachelor’s Degree or training in Marketing, Business, or related field Experience: Minimum 2 years’ experience in marketing, preferably in Fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG), distribution, or sales-driven environments Skills Required:
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills
  • Practical understanding of field marketing and consumer behavior
  • Basic reporting and data tracking skills
  • High ethical standards and professional conduct

7. PERFORMANCE EXPECTATIONS

The Marketing Officer will be evaluated based on:
  • Brand visibility and merchandising standards
  • Effectiveness of marketing campaigns and activities
  • Contribution to sales growth and market expansion
  • Quality and timeliness of market intelligence reports
  • Customer engagement and relationship management
  • Professional discipline and teamwork

8. EVALUATION & SELECTION FACTORS

  • Relevant Experience – 30%
  • Marketing & Business Understanding – 40%
  • Behavioural Competence & Integrity – 30%

9. APPLICATION PROCEDURE

Interested candidates should submit: a cover letter, CV, copy of National ID, and relevant Certificates. Subject of email: MARKETING OFFICER – G3-BIZ Ltd Send to:info@g3-bizltd.com Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. The HOD HR/Admin G3-Biz Ltd Cameroon

We understand that running a business requires continuous learning and improvement, which is why we offer free consultation to both new and established customers through FONGANG's Academy. This initiative helps business owners streamline operations, improve sales, and maximize growth potential.  

At FONGANG's Academy, we provide business owners with tailored insights and actionable strategies to:

 
  • Streamline operations: Optimize your workflow and enhance efficiency, reducing unnecessary costs and improving productivity.

  • Financial planning and management: Learn how to manage cash flow effectively, create robust financial plans, and make informed decisions for long-term success.
